concept_name: Electric Field due to an Electric Dipole
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics, Ch.1 Electric Charges and Fields (§1.9 Electric Dipole, §1.10 Electric Field due to a Dipole) — table-of-contents + Indian-anchor reference only; teaching authored from first principles. HC Verma consulted for SEQUENCE (define the dipole and its moment p → field at an axial point by superposition → field at an equatorial point → compare axial=2×equatorial → the 1/r³ falloff → the field-line map), never copied. The atomic idea: a dipole's field anywhere is just the SUPERPOSITION of the two point-charge fields E+ and E−; on the axis they reinforce along p (E≈2kp/r³), on the equator the survivors add opposite to p (E≈kp/r³), and because the two opposite charges nearly cancel at distance the field dies as 1/r³ — faster than a single charge's 1/r². Built for reviewer Asmi Singh; new field_3d scenario dipole_field. Conceptual-only (no mode_overrides), EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; misconceptions confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a).

real_world_anchor.primary: A water molecule is a tiny permanent electric dipole: the oxygen end is slightly negative and the two hydrogen ends slightly positive, so it carries a built-in dipole moment p. Every glass of water is full of these little dipoles, and the field each one makes around itself is exactly the dipole field we are drawing here.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Inside a microwave oven, the electric field flips billions of times a second. The water dipoles in your food try to keep turning to line up with it, and all that twisting is what heats the food. The torque that flips them and the field they make are two sides of the same dipole.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: Put an insulator — a slab of plastic or glass — into an electric field and its molecules stretch into countless tiny dipoles. The dipole field of each one adds up to the way a dielectric weakens the field inside it, the idea behind every capacitor with a slab in it.

r.name: Distance from the dipole centre to the field point

theta.name: Angle of the field point from the dipole axis

E_general.formula: (k * p / (r * r * r)) * sqrt(1 + 3 * cos(theta) * cos(theta))

formulas.dipole_moment: p = q × 2a, a vector pointing from −q to +q
formulas.superposition: E_net = E+ + E−  (the two point-charge fields added as vectors)
formulas.axial_field: E_axial = 2 k p / r³, along p (far field, r ≫ a)
formulas.equatorial_field: E_equatorial = k p / r³, opposite to p (far field, r ≫ a)
formulas.general_point: E = (k p / r³) √(1 + 3cos²θ); θ measured from the axis

constraints: The dipole field at ANY point is just the vector sum of the two point-charge fields E+ (away from +q) and E− (toward −q) — there is no new law, only superposition
On the axis the two fields reinforce along p; on the equator their components along p cancel and the components opposite p survive — so axial points along p and equatorial points opposite p
At the same distance the axial field is exactly TWICE the equatorial field (far field)
Both axial and equatorial fields fall off as 1/r³ — faster than a single point charge's 1/r² — because the +q and −q nearly cancel when seen from far away
Only the dipole moment p = q·2a matters at a distance, not q and a separately: a bigger charge or a wider separation give the same field if p is the same

aha_moment.statement: A dipole's field dies as 1/r³ — faster than a single charge.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: Watch the net arrow at three growing distances collapse far quicker than a single charge would; the near + and − fields nearly erase each other at range.

tts_sentences.text_en: An electric dipole is just two equal and opposite charges, plus q and minus q, held a small distance apart.
tts_sentences.text_hi: electric dipole बस दो equal और opposite charges होते हैं — plus q और minus q — जो थोड़ी दूरी पर रखे जाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: electric dipole అంటే కేవలం రెండు equal మరియు opposite charges — plus q మరియు minus q — కొంచెం distance లో ఉంచబడిన వాటి జంట.

tts_sentences.text_en: We capture it in one quantity: the dipole moment p. Its size is charge times separation, q times two a.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हम इसे एक quantity में समेटते हैं: dipole moment p। इसकी magnitude होती है charge गुना separation, यानी q times two a।
tts_sentences.text_te: మనం దీన్ని ఒక quantity లో చెప్తాం: dipole moment p. దాని size అంటే charge times separation, q times two a.

tts_sentences.text_en: p is a vector. By convention it points from the negative charge to the positive charge.
tts_sentences.text_hi: p एक vector है। convention के अनुसार यह negative charge से positive charge की तरफ point करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: p అనేది ఒక vector. convention ప్రకారం ఇది negative charge నుంచి positive charge వైపు point చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Our question for the whole lesson: what electric field does this little pair set up in the space around it?
tts_sentences.text_hi: पूरे lesson का हमारा सवाल यह है: यह छोटा pair अपने चारों तरफ की space में कैसा electric field बनाता है?
tts_sentences.text_te: మొత్తం lesson లో మన ప్రశ్న ఇది: ఈ చిన్న pair చుట్టూ ఉన్న space లో ఎలాంటి electric field ఏర్పరుస్తుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: Take a point P out along the axis, on the line through both charges. Find the field by superposition.
tts_sentences.text_hi: axis के साथ, दोनों charges की line पर एक point P लो। field को superposition से निकालते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: axis వెంట, రెండు charges ను కలిపే line పై ఒక point P తీసుకోండి. field ను superposition ద్వారా కనుగొందాం.

tts_sentences.text_en: The near plus q pushes outward — a strong red arrow. The far minus q pulls back — a weaker blue arrow.
tts_sentences.text_hi: पास का plus q बाहर की तरफ push करता है — एक strong red arrow। दूर का minus q वापस pull करता है — एक weaker blue arrow।
tts_sentences.text_te: దగ్గర ఉన్న plus q బయటికి push చేస్తుంది — ఒక strong red arrow. దూరంలో ఉన్న minus q వెనక్కి pull చేస్తుంది — ఒక weaker blue arrow.

tts_sentences.text_en: They lie along the same line, so the green net field points along p, the way p points.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दोनों एक ही line पर हैं, इसलिए green net field p की दिशा में point करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండూ ఒకే line పై ఉన్నాయి, కాబట్టి green net field p వైపు point చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Work it out far away and the axial field is two k p over r cubed.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दूर वाले point के लिए calculate करो तो axial field होती है two k p over r cubed।
tts_sentences.text_te: దూరంగా ఉన్న point కి calculate చేస్తే axial field two k p over r cubed అవుతుంది.

scene_composition.text: predict: which way will E point?

tts_sentences.text_en: Now move P out sideways, onto the perpendicular bisector. Before I draw it — predict which way the field points.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब P को sideways, perpendicular bisector पर ले जाओ। मैं draw करने से पहले — predict करो, field किस direction में point करेगी?
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు P ని sideways గా, perpendicular bisector పైకి తీసుకువెళ్ళు. నేను draw చేయడానికి ముందు — predict చేయి, field ఏ వైపు point చేస్తుందో?

tts_sentences.text_en: E plus and E minus are now equal in length but tilted. Their parts pointing up and down cancel.
tts_sentences.text_hi: E plus और E minus अब length में equal हैं लेकिन tilted हैं। उनके up और down वाले components cancel हो जाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: E plus మరియు E minus ఇప్పుడు length లో equal గా ఉన్నాయి కానీ tilted గా ఉన్నాయి. వాటి up మరియు down వైపు ఉన్న parts cancel అయిపోతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: What survives points sideways — opposite to p, back toward the negative charge's side.
tts_sentences.text_hi: जो बचता है वह sideways point करता है — p के opposite, negative charge की तरफ वापस।
tts_sentences.text_te: మిగిలేది sideways గా point చేస్తుంది — p కి opposite గా, negative charge వైపు వెళ్ళి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Far away the equatorial field is k p over r cubed, pointing opposite to p.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दूर equatorial field होती है k p over r cubed, जो p के opposite direction में point करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: దూరంగా equatorial field k p over r cubed అవుతుంది, p కి opposite direction లో point చేస్తూ.

misconception_watch.belief: The dipole field always points from + to − like p, everywhere around the dipole
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Show the equatorial net arrow pointing OPPOSITE to p while the axial one points along p.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Direction depends on where you stand: along p on the axis, opposite p on the equator.

tts_sentences.text_en: Put the two cases side by side at the same distance from the centre.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दोनों cases को centre से same distance पर side by side रखो।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు cases ను centre నుంచి same distance లో side by side పెట్టండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The axial arrow is exactly twice as long as the equatorial one — same r, double the field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: axial arrow की length equatorial arrow से exactly double है — same r, double field।
tts_sentences.text_te: axial arrow పొడవు equatorial arrow కంటే exactly రెండు రెట్లు ఉంది — same r, double field.

tts_sentences.text_en: And they point differently: axial along p, equatorial opposite p.
tts_sentences.text_hi: और दोनों अलग-अलग direction में point करते हैं: axial, p के साथ; equatorial, p के opposite।
tts_sentences.text_te: మరియు రెండూ వేరు వేరు వైపు point చేస్తాయి: axial, p వైపు; equatorial, p కి opposite గా.

tts_sentences.text_en: One clean rule to remember: on the axis the dipole field is twice as strong as on the equator.
tts_sentences.text_hi: याद रखने के लिए एक simple rule: axis पर dipole field equator से exactly दोगुनी होती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: గుర్తుంచుకోవడానికి ఒక simple rule: axis పై dipole field equator కంటే exactly రెండు రెట్లు strong గా ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the net arrow at three growing distances. What happens to its length as I step outward?
tts_sentences.text_hi: तीन बढ़ती हुई distances पर net arrow को देखो। जैसे-जैसे मैं बाहर जाता हूँ, इसकी length का क्या होता है?
tts_sentences.text_te: మూడు పెరుగుతున్న distances వద్ద net arrow ను చూడండి. నేను బయటికి వెళ్ళే కొద్దీ దాని length కి ఏం జరుగుతుందో?

tts_sentences.text_en: It collapses — far faster than a single charge would. The dipole field goes as one over r cubed.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह rapidly collapse हो जाता है — single charge से कहीं तेज। dipole field जाती है one over r cubed की तरह।
tts_sentences.text_te: అది collapse అవుతుంది — single charge కంటే చాలా వేగంగా. dipole field one over r cubed లాగా తగ్గిపోతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Here is why: from far away the plus and the minus almost line up and nearly cancel each other.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसका कारण यह है: दूर से देखने पर plus और minus लगभग line up हो जाते हैं और एक-दूसरे को nearly cancel कर देते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: దీనికి కారణమేమిటంటే: దూరం నుంచి చూస్తే plus మరియు minus దాదాపు line up అయి ఒకదానికొకటి nearly cancel చేసుకుంటాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: What is left is a faint leftover that fades as r cubed, not r squared. A dipole hides quickly at a distance.
tts_sentences.text_hi: जो बचता है वह एक faint leftover है जो r cubed की तरह fade होता है, r squared की तरह नहीं। distance पर dipole जल्दी hide हो जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మిగిలేది ఒక faint leftover, అది r cubed లాగా fade అవుతుంది, r squared లాగా కాదు. distance లో dipole చాలా వేగంగా hide అయిపోతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: A dipole's field falls off like a point charge, as 1/r²
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Show the net arrow shrinking across three distances much faster than a 1/r² arrow would.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: A dipole field dies as 1/r³ — faster than 1/r² — because the opposite charges nearly cancel far away.

tts_sentences.text_en: Join all those net arrows into continuous field lines and you get the dipole's map.
tts_sentences.text_hi: उन सभी net arrows को जोड़ो continuous field lines में, तो dipole का map मिलता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: అన్ని net arrows ను కలిపి continuous field lines గా మార్చండి, అప్పుడు dipole యొక్క map వస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Every line leaves the positive charge and curves around to land on the negative charge.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर line positive charge से निकलती है और curve करके negative charge पर land करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి line positive charge నుంచి బయలుదేరి curve చేసి negative charge పై land అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The lines crowd most near the axis — that is where the field is strongest, just as we found.
tts_sentences.text_hi: lines सबसे ज्यादा axis के पास crowd होती हैं — वहाँ field सबसे strong है, ठीक जैसा हमने पाया।
tts_sentences.text_te: lines axis దగ్గర అత్యంత crowd గా ఉంటాయి — అక్కడే field అత్యంత strong గా ఉంటుంది, మనం కనుగొన్నట్లుగానే.

tts_sentences.text_en: In general the strength is k p over r cubed times root of one plus three cosine squared theta.
tts_sentences.text_hi: सामान्य रूप में strength होती है k p over r cubed times root of one plus three cosine squared theta।
tts_sentences.text_te: సాధారణంగా strength అంటే k p over r cubed times root of one plus three cosine squared theta.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now you drive it. Move the point P around the dipole with the angle slider and watch the net field swing.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आप चलाइए। angle slider से point P को dipole के चारों तरफ move करो और net field को swing होते देखो।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీరు నడిపించండి. angle slider తో point P ని dipole చుట్టూ move చేయండి మరియు net field swing అవడం చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: At zero degrees you are on the axis — the field is strong and points along p.
tts_sentences.text_hi: zero degrees पर आप axis पर हो — field strong है और p के साथ point करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: zero degrees వద్ద మీరు axis పై ఉన్నారు — field strong గా ఉంటుంది మరియు p వైపు point చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: At ninety degrees you are on the equator — about half as strong, pointing opposite p.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ninety degrees पर आप equator पर हो — लगभग half strong, p के opposite direction में point करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ninety degrees వద్ద మీరు equator పై ఉన్నారు — సుమారు half strong గా, p కి opposite direction లో point చేస్తూ.

tts_sentences.text_en: Slide r outward and feel how fast it fades; raise q and the whole field grows. One dipole, one pattern.
tts_sentences.text_hi: r को outward slide करो और देखो कितनी जल्दी field fade होती है; q बढ़ाओ और पूरी field बढ़ जाती है। एक dipole, एक pattern।
tts_sentences.text_te: r ని outward slide చేయండి మరియు field ఎంత వేగంగా fade అవుతుందో చూడండి; q పెంచండి మరియు మొత్తం field పెరుగుతుంది. ఒక dipole, ఒక pattern.

STATE_2.label: Axial point — superpose E+ and E−
